name: Natural Park of Cabo de Gata – Chacona Cord Trail (PR-A 96)
name_original: Parque Natural de Cabo de Gata – Sendero de la Cuerda de la Chacona (PR-A 96)
region_intro: Hiking around Mojácar offers diverse landscapes, blending coastal beauty with mountainous terrain. The region is characterized by a rugged shoreline, the foothills of the Sierra Cabrera mountains, and the unique geological formations of the nearby Cabo de Gata Natural Park. Cultivated lands, such as the Orchards of Mojácar, provide a contrast to the natural coastal and mountain environments. Best hiking trails around Mojácar The most popular hiking route is Natural Park of Cabo de Gata – Chacona Cord Trail (PR-A 96), an 8.0 miles (12.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 31 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the unique geological formations within the Cabo de Gata Natural Park. Another top favourite among local hikers is The Orchards of Mojacar - circular (SL-A 184), an easy 3.4 miles (5.5 km) path. This trail leads through cultivated lands with orange and olive trees, showcasing the village's farming past. Local hikers also love the Pirulico Tower – Torre del Pirulico loop from Playa Venta del Bancal, a 5.6 miles (9.1 km) trail leading through coastal landscapes and past the historic Torre del Pirulico, often completed in about 2 hours 25 minutes. Hiking around Mojácar is defined by coastal paths, mountainous terrain, and cultivated orchards. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate climbs. The routes in Mojácar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 1500 hikers have used komoot to explore Mojácar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Mojácar?
A: Mojácar offers a diverse network of over 50 hiking trails, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These routes explore the region's unique blend of coastal beauty, mountainous terrain, and historical charm.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Mojácar's hiking trails?
A: Hikers in Mojácar can experience a wide variety of terrain. You'll find rugged coastal paths offering stunning sea views, trails winding through the foothills of the Sierra Cabrera mountains, and routes exploring the unique geological formations of the nearby Cabo de Gata Natural Park. There are also gentler walks through cultivated lands, such as the Orchards of Mojácar, showcasing the village's farming past.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking options in Mojácar?
A: Yes, Mojácar has several easy trails perfect for families. One excellent option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983805112/the-orchards-of-mojacar-circular-sl-a-184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Orchards of Mojacar - circular (SL-A 184)&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.4-mile (5.5 km) path that takes you through cultivated lands with orange and olive trees. This trail is relatively flat and offers a pleasant, gentle walk for all ages.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Mojácar?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Mojácar are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40375411" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pirulico Tower – Torre del Pirulico loop from Playa Venta del Bancal&lt;/a&gt;, a 5.6-mile (9.1 km) route that combines coastal scenery with a historical landmark. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983805112/the-orchards-of-mojacar-circular-sl-a-184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Orchards of Mojacar - circular (SL-A 184)&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy loop through agricultural landscapes.

Q: What historical landmarks or viewpoints can I see while hiking in Mojácar?
A: Mojácar's trails often lead to fascinating historical sites and offer breathtaking views. You can hike to the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4067165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pirulico Tower&lt;/a&gt;, a 13th-15th century coastal watchtower, or explore &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5060861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mojácar Old Town&lt;/a&gt; itself, with its Moorish architecture and panoramic vistas from Mirador de la Plaza Nueva. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3291513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views from the Granatilla viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; also offer spectacular coastal scenery.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly hiking trails in Mojácar?
A: Mojácar is generally welcoming to dogs on its hiking trails, especially in natural areas like the Sierra Cabrera mountains and coastal paths. While specific restrictions might apply in certain protected zones or on beaches during peak season, most trails are suitable for well-behaved dogs on a leash. Always ensure you carry water for your pet and clean up after them.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Mojácar?
A: The best time for hiking in Mojácar is typically during the cooler months, from autumn (September to November) through spring (March to May). During these periods, the weather is mild and pleasant, ideal for exploring the trails. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes more advisable if visiting then.

Q: Are there any trails that offer spectacular sea and mountain views?
A: Yes, many trails in Mojácar provide stunning sea and mountain views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6882267" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Above Cerro del Judío – Mojácar Old Town loop from Mojácar&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, offers elevated perspectives of both the coastline and the Sierra Cabrera foothills. Coastal routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5288763" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pirulico Tower – Torre del Pirulico loop from Macenas&lt;/a&gt; also deliver breathtaking Mediterranean vistas.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Mojácar?
A: The hiking experience in Mojácar is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 150 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, the stunning coastal and mountain views, and the opportunity to combine outdoor activity with exploring historical sites like the Torre del Pirulico and Mojácar Pueblo. The variety of trails, from easy walks to moderate climbs, is also a highlight.

Q: Are there any trails within the Cabo de Gata Natural Park near Mojácar?
A: Yes, the Natural Park of Cabo de Gata-Níjar is just a few kilometers from Mojácar and offers unique hiking opportunities. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984708288/natural-park-of-cabo-de-gata-chacona-cord-trail-pr-a-96" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of Cabo de Gata – Chacona Cord Trail (PR-A 96)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.0-mile (12.9 km) route within the park, allowing you to experience its distinctive geological formations and rich ecosystem.

Q: Are there options for public transport to reach trailheads in Mojácar?
A: While some trailheads might be accessible by local bus services connecting Mojácar Pueblo with the beach areas, many routes, especially those venturing into the Sierra Cabrera or more remote coastal sections, are best reached by car. Parking information is often available for popular trailheads, but checking local transport schedules for specific starting points is recommended if relying on public transport.
tips: Curious rock formation near the slope of the strait.

It was built in the second half of the 18th century and had the mission of defending the beach from pirate attacks, since it was used in great draft that the beach has near the shore to load ships with mineral, esparto grass and palm leaves.

The Torre del Pirulico, also known as the Torre Horadada or Torre del Peñón, is an ancient coastal watchtower located in Mojácar. This tower was built between the 12th and 14th centuries during the Nasrid period and was used to warn the population of threats from the sea, especially pirate attacks.

The tower has a truncated cone shape and stands 10 meters high. At its base, there is an arch that allows one to pass through it, giving it its name Torre Horadada. Entrance is through a door located approximately 7 meters high, accessible via a fixed metal staircase. From the tower's roof, one can enjoy stunning views of the sea and the surrounding landscape.
